content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

May 15, 2003

Betty Riley
10721 Shenandoah Road
Plymouth, CA 95669

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-03-044

Dear Ms. Riley:

This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the conflict of interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ Your question concerned whether you, as a planning commissioner who is also a wine grape grower, could participate in proceedings regarding a proposed change in the county's winery ordinance, which would allow on-site fresh food service in the winery tasting rooms. However, on Friday, May 9, 2003, you left a telephone message that, because your husband has recently accepted employment with the Villa Toscano winery, which already has food service and is the primary reason for the proposed change to the winery ordinance, you have decided not to vote on the proposed ordinance change when it returns to the planning commission for consideration. Therefore, your request for advice is withdrawn.² (Reg. 18329(b)(8)(F).)

For your information we have enclosed our fact sheet, "Can I Vote? Conflicts of Interest Overview." If you should have a question under the Act about a particular decision in the future, please feel free to contact us for advice.

_______________

¹ Government Code sections 81000 – 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections 18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.

² This withdrawal does not constitute advice regarding any past conduct or any issues related to promised income.

File No. W-03-044
Page No. 2

If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By: Holly B. Armstrong
Staff Counsel, Legal Division

Enclosure
HBA:jg
E:\AdviceLtrs\W-03-044

sections.question_synthetic: Whether a planning commissioner who is also a wine grape grower could participate in proceedings regarding a proposed change to the county's winery ordinance allowing on-site fresh food service, given the requestor's husband's employment with a winery affected by the proposed change.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The request for advice is withdrawn because the requestor decided not to vote on the proposed ordinance change when it returns to the planning commission, having recognized the conflict of interest created by her husband's recent employment with the primary winery affected by the proposed ordinance change.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Withdrawal letter rather than standard advice letter; no formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections present. The legal question and requestor's resolution are described in the narrative body. Document includes a disclaimer that withdrawal does not constitute advice on past conduct or promised income issues.

QUESTION: Whether a planning commissioner who is also a wine grape grower could participate in proceedings regarding a proposed change to the county's winery ordinance allowing on-site fresh food service, given the requestor's husband's employment with a winery affected by the proposed change.

CONCLUSION: The request for advice is withdrawn because the requestor decided not to vote on the proposed ordinance change when it returns to the planning commission, having recognized the conflict of interest created by her husband's recent employment with the primary winery affected by the proposed ordinance change.

embedding.summary: This is a withdrawal letter in which Betty Riley withdraws her request for FPPC advice regarding a potential conflict of interest in voting on a winery ordinance change after her husband accepted employment with Villa Toscano winery, the primary beneficiary of the proposed ordinance.
